test.notes: VAL-DPP-001 PASS (max_abs_err < 2.44e-4 for all 4 Qwen 3.5 shapes), VAL-DPP-002 PASS (all primary shapes within 5% margin). Python syntax check also passed for all src/ and tests/ files.

typecheck.command: python3 -c "import ast, os, sys; ...(all src/ and tests/ .py files)"
typecheck.notes: No syntax errors found in src/ or tests/ Python files. Project uses HIP C++ kernels which require remote compilation; hipcc build also succeeded (gemv_int4_v4.so and gemv_int4_v5.so both compiled without errors).

lint.notes: No local linting tools (flake8/pylint) are configured in the project. Python syntax compilation check passed for key modified files.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the 'GEMV Kernel' section in Architecture Notes to reflect that gemv_int4_v5 is now the default. Currently says 'gemv_int4_v4 with... LDS tree reduction'. Should read: 'gemv_int4_v5 (default, hybrid DPP+LDS) / v3 (fallback) with v_dot2_f32_f16, v_bfe_u32, cooperative reduction (t4/t8/t16 variants). v5 uses intra-wavefront shfl_down + minimal cross-wavefront LDS (row-major layout preserved for coalescing).'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gemv-dpp-e2e-integration (commit 26c4961) wired v5 as default in engine.py, but AGENTS.md still references v4 as the current GEMV kernel in its Architecture Notes section.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a step to kernel-optimization-worker skill: before investing in compute-side optimizations (DPP, shfl, vectorization), verify whether the kernel is compute-bound or bandwidth-bound. For INT4 GEMV on MI50 (857 GB/s peak HBM2), the kernel is strictly bandwidth-limited, so compute-side optimizations (DPP reduction) cannot improve wall-clock time. A quick roofline analysis (arithmetic intensity × peak bandwidth vs peak compute) would identify this upfront.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gemv-dpp-reduction worker spent iteration time on DPP implementation that delivered no measured speedup. Worker documented: 'the kernel is memory-bandwidth bound and LDS reduction is not the bottleneck.' skillFeedback explicitly requested this addition.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that TP=4 E2E tests (test_gemv_v5_e2e.py, similar patterns) take ~800s to complete on 4x MI50. The default 600s timeout is insufficient. Workers should use a 900s+ timeout for SSH Docker commands running full TP=4 E2E tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: gemv-dpp-e2e-integration worker skillFeedback: 'The test script timeout (600s) is too short for the full TP=4 E2E test which takes ~800s. Consider increasing the default or documenting this in the skill.'
